untranslatable_reason: The Jegadeesh-Titman momentum strategy requires ranking securities across a universe, decile portfolio construction, and multi-month formation/holding periods with dynamic rebalancing. This cannot be expressed as simple indicator-based entry/exit conditions on a single security. The strategy operates at the portfolio construction and relative ranking level, not at the individual price/indicator level. Formation period J and holding period K parameters (3-12 months each) require explicit portfolio management logic outside the scope of indicator-based rules.
translation_notes: This is a cross-sectional, relative-value strategy requiring universe-level ranking and portfolio construction. The backtest engine would need dedicated portfolio management logic to (1) rank all securities by returns over J months, (2) form decile portfolios, (3) allocate to top/bottom deciles, and (4) rebalance every K months. A single-security rule set cannot capture this strategy.
